project.name: EchoSpectrum
project.mission: Provide a receive-only and synthetic-only spectrum observability layer for civic signal integrity testing, evidence-shape validation, and safe cross-product simulations.

governance.defaultSafetyPosture: Receive-only and synthetic-only. No transmission, disruption, spoofing, overpowering, exploitation, interference, or operational RF effects are permitted.
requiresHumanApprovalFor: any change that affects data ingestion boundaries
any change that affects civic escalation workflows
any integration with SignalForge, ETS, Christina, OpsHelm, or Lantern-Civic
any movement beyond synthetic simulation or passive observation modeling

SignalForge.role: Ingest and route EchoSpectrum synthetic/passive signal events through shared Lantern event contracts.
SignalForge.notes: SignalForge receives structured observability events, not disruptive RF capabilities.

ETS.role: Verify provenance, evidence shape, replayability, and trust status for generated or observed events.
ETS.notes: ETS is the trust anchor and must preserve uncertainty states.

Christina.role: Route safe issues, approval requests, and human-review workflows.
Christina.notes: Christina may draft and route work but may not approve unsafe escalation alone.

OpsHelm.role: Classify operational incidents and support human-reviewed escalation workflows.
OpsHelm.notes: OpsHelm consumes verified events only after SignalForge/ETS handling.

Lantern-Civic.role: Use verified EchoSpectrum events for civic resilience modeling and human-reviewed situational awareness.
Lantern-Civic.notes: Lantern-Civic interprets civic impact; EchoSpectrum only produces passive/synthetic observability signals.

local.notes: Local development and synthetic simulation only.

christina.issueRoutingRole: Supporting-service work for passive/synthetic signal observability and manifest alignment.
